summary: 2 Dogs Treats is a Boston-based direct-to-consumer brand that makes single-ingredient, human-grade dog treats and chews in small batches (Just Chicken, Just Beef, fish skins, organic sweet potato, Summit chews), plus a Build-a-Box and gift boxes. It serves health-conscious dog owners who want full ingredient transparency, especially those whose dogs have allergies, sensitivities, or picky palates, and who will pay a premium for treats they can trust. Revenue is self-serve e-commerce with an available subscription, backed by a full satisfaction-guarantee refund.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Activate a new subscriber or first-time buyer by pre-framing the 'aha' moment (dog goes wild + owner flips the bag to one ingredient) and driving the first or second order.
campaigns.trigger: Newsletter signup or first order placed; send instantly.
campaigns.segment: New contacts (newsletter signups and first-time buyers); branch on whether they've purchased yet.
campaigns.rationale: There's no welcome flow today beyond a likely-thin Shopify default, and welcome emails are the most-opened a brand sends. This is the fastest way to set the single-ingredient philosophy and remove first-purchase hesitation with the satisfaction guarantee.

emails.purpose: Warm founder welcome, set the single-ingredient promise, and pre-frame the first-delivery 'aha' moment so the customer knows what to watch for.
emails.subject: Welcome to the pack — flip the bag, every time
emails.preview: One ingredient. That's the whole list. Here's why that matters for your pup.
keyPoints: Founder-led hello from Mike & Riley's story; warm, neighbor-not-marketer tone
The promise: every bag is ONE ingredient — no fillers, preservatives, or mystery meat, made in our Boston kitchen
Pre-frame the aha: 'When your order lands, watch your pup's reaction, then flip the bag and read the whole ingredient list in two seconds'
Risk reversal: full refund if your dog doesn't love it — zero risk to try
Reply-to is a real person at woof@2dogstreats.com

campaigns.name: Newsletter Subscriber to First Purchase
campaigns.objective: Convert engaged newsletter subscribers who haven't yet bought into a first order, using the satisfaction guarantee as the brand's trial substitute.
campaigns.trigger: Subscriber on list 5+ days with no purchase; behavioral nudge on product-page or reviews-page visits.
campaigns.segment: Non-purchasing subscribers; branch by browsed protein/category if known (allergy/sensitive, senior-soft, gifting).
campaigns.rationale: Newsletter-to-first-purchase is a named primary goal and the core revenue play; with no free trial, the deciding email must neutralize the #1 objection (price premium) with verifiable transparency, 1,162 reviews at 97% positive, and the no-risk refund.

emails.purpose: Handle the price objection head-on with proof and the guarantee — the single most decisive email for turning a curious subscriber into a buyer.
emails.subject: Why $20 for chicken? Open the bag and you'll get it
emails.preview: 1,162 reviews, 97% positive, and a refund if your dog says no. Here's the honest math.
keyPoints: Name the objection plainly: store-brand is cheaper, here's exactly what your dog isn't eating in ours
Human-grade, USA-sourced, single ingredient you can verify by reading the label — no imported mystery meat
Social proof: 1,162 reviews / 97% positive, 'vet noticed her coat,' 'same enthusiasm 9 years later'
De-risk: satisfaction guarantee = full refund if your dog doesn't love it
Nudge to free shipping over $66 with a Build-a-Box to sample multiple proteins

campaigns.name: Subscription Conversion & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Lift LTV by converting happy one-time buyers into subscribers and expanding them into new proteins and Summit chews.
campaigns.trigger: ~14 days after first order delivered (enough time for the aha to land, before the bag runs low).
campaigns.segment: One-time buyers not on subscription; branch high-usage/multi-dog (push subscribe + bulk) vs single-protein buyers (push variety).
campaigns.rationale: One-time buyers lapse silently and dogs reorder every 4-8 weeks — converting the post-aha buyer to subscribe-and-save is the highest-leverage retention win, and a fast one given subscription tooling already exists.

emails.purpose: Catch the buyer right after their dog's first rave reaction and make reordering automatic — frame subscription as never running out plus a try-a-new-protein expansion.
emails.subject: Did Riley- I mean YOUR pup- already inhale the bag?
emails.preview: Never run out, save on every order, and let your dog try a new protein on us.
keyPoints: Lead with the moment: if the first bag was a hit, you've already passed the hardest test
Subscription = never run out between the 4-8 week reorder cycle, with savings on every shipment
No contract, change or cancel anytime — same satisfaction guarantee applies
Expansion nudge: rotate proteins to beat flavor fatigue (loved Just Beef? add Just Turkey) or add a Summit chew
Multi-dog/bulk angle for high-volume households

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed one-time buyers and dormant subscribers before they default back to supermarket treats.
campaigns.trigger: 60-90 days since last order with no reorder, or a cancelled subscription.
campaigns.segment: Lapsed buyers (reference the protein they bought most); separately, churned subscribers; suppress to sunset if non-responsive.
campaigns.rationale: Lapsed buyers are a known, warm revenue gap and win-back recovers 5-15% of inactives; a 'your pup misses these' nudge that reminds them of the clean-ingredient peace of mind is quick to ship and protects deliverability.

emails.purpose: Reopen the relationship by reminding the lapsed buyer of the core value their dog loved, with a frictionless one-click reorder of their go-to protein.
emails.subject: Is your pup giving you the side-eye yet?
emails.preview: It's been a while. Your dog's favorite single-ingredient treat is one click away.
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt 'we miss your pup' open in the brand's neighborly voice
Remind of the value: still one ingredient, still human-grade, still made small-batch in Boston
Personalize to the protein they bought most last time for a one-click reorder
Reassurance the satisfaction guarantee still stands if they want to try something new
Soft check-in: hit reply if your dog's needs changed (new allergy, senior, picky) and we'll recommend

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build authority on clean dog nutrition without discounting, keeping the list engaged between orders.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding and route dormant contacts to win-back instead.
campaigns.rationale: Blog content exists but there's no evidence of a regular value email; an always-on newsletter is the retention engine that keeps the brand present across the 4-8 week reorder gap and reinforces the ingredient-transparency authority that justifies the premium.

emails.purpose: Establish the repeatable hero edition: one genuinely useful ingredient-education idea, lightly tied to a product, that builds the habit of opening 2 Dogs emails.
emails.subject: The 3 words hiding on most 'healthy' treat labels
emails.preview: If you can't pronounce it, your dog probably shouldn't eat it. Here's how to read a label.
keyPoints: One useful idea per send: how to actually read a dog-treat ingredient label and spot fillers/preservatives
80% value / 20% soft offer — teach first, then point to our single-ingredient range as the easy alternative
Recurring habit section: 'This issue's pup-nutrition tip'
Compliant framing — 'supports a healthy coat,' never disease-cure claims
Single primary CTA to the guide; reply invited with your dog's label questions
